economic_notes: 16 government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method including hydroelectric energy partnerships (Ouiatchouan, Mistassini), Namunashu SEC, Shipiss inc., Groupe PEK, Développement Piekuakami Ilnuatsh, and others. Total GBE revenues approximately $38.8M. Mashteuiasth 2001 agreement with Hydro-Québec (7.30% of Manouane project) for 50 years to 2051.
notes: French-language financial statements for Quebec First Nation. The text is cut off at Note 27 (revenue transfers) so full revenue line breakdown is incomplete. 16 entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs) including major hydroelectric partnerships. Long-term debt of $58.7M primarily related to hydroelectric project financing through Investissement Québec and First Nations Finance Authority. Restricted cash includes Band Funds ($7.03M) held in trust by Government of Canada under Indian Act s.62-69, plus CMHC housing reserves ($372K). Total long-term debt includes Art.95 housing debt ($1.18M) plus general LTD ($58.69M). Prior year audited by a different auditor (predecessor). Remuneration schedule noted as unaudited annex but values not extracted from provided text.
